one_liner: Time-series BOCPD changepoint detection to medical AI post-deployment performance drift monitoring
problem: Medical AI performance silently degrades after deployment but existing monitoring lacks statistically valid online changepoint detection. BOCPD/CUSUM is mature in industrial time series but never applied to medical AI performance stream monitoring.
approach: 1.Simulate deployment: train on NIH-CXR14 use VinDr-CXR as drift set construct mixed stream. 2.Attach BOCPD (Adams and MacKay 2007) to prediction probability stream detect drift changepoints. 3.Compare against PSI and no-guarantee baseline monitors.
why_new: BOCPD (Adams and MacKay 2007) mature in industrial QC; ML deployment monitoring has Alibi-Detect but medical imaging AI changepoint-based performance monitoring papers nearly absent. Unlike MedAD-FailMap (single-image anomaly) this targets system-level temporal drift.

one_liner: Adversarial ML randomized smoothing certified radius to MR acquisition noise certifiable uncertainty bounds
problem: Randomized smoothing (Cohen 2019) provides certified L2 perturbation radii but medical image noise is structured (Rician MR noise) not isotropic Gaussian. Nobody has ported certified robustness to medical imaging noise models.
approach: 1.Model MR Rician noise as perturbation model apply randomized smoothing variant with Rician base distribution. 2.Verify on BraTS FLAIR that small certified radius correlates with larger segmentation error. 3.Compare against Gaussian randomized smoothing baseline.
why_new: Randomized smoothing (Cohen 2019, Salman 2019) mature in adversarial ML; medical UQ has almost no certified radius framework. Non-Gaussian smoothing (Dvijotham 2020) exists but has no medical applications.
source_method: certified robustness randomized smoothing (Cohen et al. ICML 2019; Dvijotham 2020 non-Gaussian smoothing)

one_liner: Information bottleneck IB optimized for ECE target for calibration-aware feature compression in medical UQ G2 boundary case
problem: High-dimensional imaging features contain redundancy irrelevant to uncertainty. IB theory suggests optimal representations should compress irrelevant information but IB has never been optimized for reducing calibration error in medical UQ.
approach: 1.Insert IB layer before classifier head (VIB but loss objective changed to minimize ECE not cross-entropy). 2.Test calibration-aware IB vs standard VIB and temperature scaling on HAM10000/CheXpert. 3.Ablate beta trade-off on calibration vs accuracy.
why_new: Distinct from excluded Q-VIB SOTA-chasing: does not optimize AUC only ECE; hypothesis is IB as calibration mechanism not accuracy booster. Boundary close to MedSeg-UQ dead-end exclusion; flagged for G2 careful review.
source_method: information bottleneck IB (Tishby et al. 1999; Alemi 2017 VIB) optimized for ECE target

one_liner: Prediction market LMSR log market scoring rule to dynamic multi-model medical AI uncertainty aggregation
problem: Clinical multi-model ensembles average softmax outputs without accounting for information novelty across models. Prediction market LMSR weights models by new information contributed but has never been applied to medical AI uncertainty aggregation.
approach: 1.Apply LMSR mechanism: each model sequentially updates market price (posterior) weighted by KL divergence from current market. 2.Compare LMSR vs simple average vs BMA on HAM10000/BraTS using ECE and Brier score. 3.Verify LMSR advantage increases with model diversity.
why_new: LMSR (Hanson 2002, Pennock 2010) mature in economics and forecast aggregation; medical AI ensembles have zero applications. Distinction from BMA: dynamic information-increment weighting vs fixed prior-based weighting.
source_method: prediction market LMSR (Hanson 2002; Pennock and Wellman 2010)
